extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by and against each entity: I counted only the “Against” rows, namely criminal proceedings of 4 against Promoters, 0 against the Company, 0 against Directors and 0 against Subsidiaries; statutory/regulatory proceedings of 1 against the Company and 1 against Promoters; and tax proceedings of 24 against the Company plus 9 against Subsidiaries. Tax matters were kept separate from regulatory proceedings, and all “By” rows were excluded. The quantified aggregate against the Company and Subsidiaries is the stated Company amount of ₹497.58 million plus the Subsidiaries amount of ₹52.31 million, totaling ₹549.89 million; at the latest date shown, March 31, 2025, contingent liabilities use the stated Total of ₹554.60 million, not a re-sum. The concentration figures are company-wide top-customer percentages of revenue from operations for the most recent full fiscal year, Fiscal 2025: top 5 customers 93.97% and top 10 customers 96.25%. Litigation and contingent-liability tables use ₹ million; conversion to crore is by dividing by 10, so ₹549.89 million = ₹54.989 crore and ₹554.60 million = ₹55.46 crore.
